executive_summary: Northern Star Resources is a multi-asset Australian gold producer with ~1,650 koz annual production guidance for FY26, anchored by KCGM (Kalgoorlie), Yandal (Jundee and Thunderbox) and Pogo (Alaska). FY26 production guidance has been revised down twice to 1,600–1,700 koz and AISC guidance revised up to A$2,700–2,800/oz, primarily driven by peak capital and operational disruption associated with the KCGM 26 Mtpa mill expansion currently commissioning. The balance sheet remains robust with net cash of A$293m as at December 2025, providing adequate runway through the expansion period. The investment thesis is a transition story: the bull case depends critically on confirmation of KCGM throughput post-commissioning, while the structural cost drag from Yandal operations represents a persistent headwind regardless of KCGM performance.

investment_view.bull_case: KCGM mill expansion achieves or exceeds 26 Mtpa nameplate throughput in H2 FY26 or early FY27, driving a step-change in group production to well above 1,700 koz and collapsing group AISC materially below A$2,500/oz. Sustained gold prices above A$4,000/oz deliver margins exceeding A$1,500/oz, generating substantial free cash flow, re-rating on P/NAV and potential for capital returns. Reserve life of ~13 years at 1.5 g/t underpins long-dated optionality.
investment_view.base_case: KCGM mill ramps up through CY2026 but faces teething delays, with group FY26 production at the low end of revised guidance (~1,600 koz) and AISC at the high end (~A$2,800/oz). FY27 shows a partial improvement as KCGM throughput builds, partially offset by continued Yandal structural cost drag. Net cash position is maintained. The stock re-rates modestly as throughput evidence accumulates but does not fully close the discount to intrinsic value until sustained mill performance is demonstrated.
investment_view.bear_case: KCGM mill expansion encounters further mechanical, geotechnical or processing issues, requiring a third guidance downgrade and prolonged elevated capital spend. Yandal continues as a structural AISC drag. A gold price correction to A$3,500/oz narrows margins to ~A$750/oz, materially reducing cash generation and potentially eroding the net cash position. Continued guidance misses damage management credibility and compress valuation multiples.
what_must_be_true: KCGM mill expansion achieves sustained throughput materially above the pre-expansion rate and progresses towards 26 Mtpa nameplate capacity without further significant disruption
Group AISC normalises below A$2,500/oz as KCGM expansion capital peaks and rolls off, moving through the cost cycle
Yandal operations maintain positive cash margins despite being a structural AISC drag, with no material reserve depletion surprise
Pogo (Alaska) delivers stable underground production without material cost blowouts or US jurisdictional issues
Gold price (AUD) remains above A$3,000/oz to sustain positive AISC margins across the portfolio
Balance sheet net cash position is preserved through the expansion period without requiring equity issuance
Reserve replacement via exploration maintains or extends the ~13-year reserve life
investment_view.monitoring_trigger: KCGM quarterly throughput rate (Mt processed) disclosed in the March 2026 and June 2026 quarterlies — any quarterly throughput materially below the implied 26 Mtpa annualised rate by end of FY26 signals the bull thesis is at risk and warrants reassessment

valuation.valuation_notes: No NAV, NPV, IRR, P/NAV or market capitalisation data available in corpus. Spot margin can be inferred from corpus: at FY26 AISC guidance midpoint of A$2,750/oz and gold spot above A$4,000/oz, the AISC margin is approximately A$1,250/oz. Under a stress-case gold price of A$3,500/oz (implied ~US$2,200/oz at AUD/USD 0.63), the margin narrows to approximately A$750/oz. At A$2,200/oz AUD-equivalent, NST would be at or below AISC breakeven. No formal valuation can be constructed from available corpus data without asset-level NPVs, capex quantum, reserve ounces by asset, or market capitalisation figures.

peer_frame.relative_valuation_comment: No peer valuation data or median multiples are available in corpus. NST is one of the largest ASX-listed gold producers by production volume. At ~1,650 koz annual production with a 13-year reserve life and net cash balance sheet, NST would typically command a premium P/NAV multiple relative to smaller Australian gold peers if expansion execution risk is resolved. The current execution risk discount associated with KCGM expansion and two guidance downgrades likely compresses the multiple relative to peers with more predictable near-term production profiles. A formal relative valuation requires current market capitalisation, peer NAVs and EV/production data not available in corpus.

top_failure_modes: KCGM mill expansion fails to achieve 26 Mtpa nameplate throughput within expected timeframe, requiring further capital and producing a third or fourth production guidance downgrade
Sustained gold price decline to below A$2,750/oz eliminates AISC margin across the group
Yandal structural cost drag proves more persistent than modelled, preventing group AISC normalisation post-KCGM expansion
Pogo (Alaska) encounters underground geotechnical, operational or US regulatory issues causing material production disruption
Capex overrun on KCGM expansion erodes net cash position and forces balance sheet remediation
Reserve replacement fails to maintain the ~13-year reserve life, signalling mine life contraction

management_assessment: Corpus evidence is insufficient for a comprehensive management assessment. The available evidence — two downward revisions to FY26 production guidance and an upward revision to AISC guidance within a single financial year — suggests that the execution of the KCGM expansion has been more disruptive than management originally guided the market to expect. This raises questions about the accuracy of the initial expansion timeline and throughput assumptions, and about the rigour of guidance setting during a complex capital project. Whether this reflects a systemic issue with management's operational delivery capabilities or a one-off execution challenge associated with a large-scale mill construction is not determinable from the corpus alone. No direct assessment of board composition, remuneration structure, track record at prior companies, or management tenure is available in the corpus. The maintenance of a net cash position through this expansion period is a positive indicator of financial discipline. Inference: management credibility is under moderate pressure during FY26 and will be assessed by the market primarily on the basis of KCGM throughput outcomes in the coming quarters.

recommendation.reason: The investment thesis is a binary-adjacent throughput confirmation event at KCGM. With the mill expansion commissioning in progress as at the analysis date, the key value-creating or value-destroying information — whether 26 Mtpa throughput is achievable — is not yet in the public domain. Two prior guidance downgrades have damaged near-term management credibility and compress the risk-reward at current pricing until throughput is confirmed. The balance sheet is adequate and the reserve life is long, but the absence of asset-level reserve, cost, capex and NAV data in the corpus, combined with the structural Yandal drag and unquantified Pogo jurisdictional risk, means the information set is insufficient to underwrite a confident investment decision. Defer pending the March 2026 quarterly report, which should provide the first material evidence of post-commissioning KCGM throughput rates. If throughput is confirmed at or above ~20 Mtpa annualised in that quarter with a clear ramp trajectory to 26 Mtpa, the base case becomes investable.

evidence.label: FY26 production guidance — revised down twice
evidence.finding: FY26 group production guidance was originally set at 1,700–1,850 koz and was revised down twice to 1,600–1,700 koz as at the December 2025 quarterly. The midpoint of revised guidance is approximately 1,650 koz.

evidence.label: FY26 AISC guidance — revised upward
evidence.finding: FY26 AISC guidance midpoint was revised up to approximately A$2,700–2,800/oz, elevated relative to a normalised cost profile, primarily driven by peak KCGM expansion capital during construction.

evidence.label: KCGM mill expansion — throughput target and commissioning status
evidence.finding: The KCGM mill expansion is targeting 26 Mtpa throughput. Commissioning was in progress as at the December 2025 quarterly. Throughput achievement has not yet been confirmed.

evidence.label: Net cash position — December 2025
evidence.finding: NST held a net cash position of A$293m as at December 2025. No significant debt maturity pressure disclosed. Funding runway considered adequate through the KCGM expansion period.

evidence.label: Reserve grade and mine life
evidence.finding: NST's Ore Reserve grade is approximately 1.5 g/t (corpus estimate) and the disclosed Ore Reserve life is approximately 13 years. The precise JORC breakdown by asset is not separately enumerated in the available corpus.
evidence.source: Corpus estimate — source document not individually identified

evidence.label: Yandal structural cost drag
evidence.finding: Yandal operations (Jundee and Thunderbox) carry a higher per-ounce cost than KCGM due to lower grade and scale, and are identified as a structural drag on group AISC that persists beyond the KCGM expansion period.
evidence.source: Corpus analysis — source document not individually identified

evidence.label: AISC margin sensitivity — spot and stress case
evidence.finding: At AISC guidance midpoint of A$2,750/oz and gold spot above A$4,000/oz, the AISC margin is approximately A$1,250/oz. Under a stress-case AUD gold price of A$3,500/oz (approximately US$2,200/oz at AUD/USD 0.63), the margin narrows to approximately A$750/oz. At A$2,200/oz AUD-equivalent, NST would be at or below AISC breakeven.
evidence.source: Corpus valuation commentary — source document not individually identified

evidence.label: Pogo — underground operation, Alaska
evidence.finding: Pogo is an underground gold mine in Alaska, USA, operated by NST. It introduces USD revenue and cost exposure and US mining jurisdiction risk. No asset-level production, cost or reserve data is available in corpus.
evidence.source: Corpus — source document not individually identified

evidence.label: Asset ownership structure
evidence.finding: NST owns and operates KCGM (Kalgoorlie), Yandal (Jundee and Thunderbox) and Pogo (Alaska). All assets are described as 100% owned and operated by NST.
evidence.source: Corpus — source document not individually identified

verdict: Northern Star Resources is a multi-asset Australian gold producer with ~1,650 koz annual production guidance for FY26, anchored by KCGM (Kalgoorlie), Yandal (Jundee and Thunderbox) and Pogo (Alaska). FY26 production guidance has been revised down twice to 1,600–1,700 koz and AISC guidance revised up to A$2,700–2,800/oz, primarily driven by peak capital and operational disruption associated with the KCGM 26 Mtpa mill expansion currently commissioning. The balance sheet remains robust with net cash of A$293m as at December 2025, providing adequate runway through the expansion period. The investment thesis is a transition story: the bull case depends critically on confirmation of KCGM throughput post-commissioning, while the structural cost drag from Yandal operations represents a persistent headwind regardless of KCGM performance.
